name: Sierpinski Fractal
description: A fractal antenna based on the Sierpinski triangle geometry, exhibiting self-similar multiband characteristics with logarithmically periodic frequency response. The fractal structure provides multiple resonances at frequencies related by the scaling factor, typically offering 3-5 distinct operating bands with similar radiation patterns.

iteration_level.name: Iteration Level
iteration_level.description: Number of fractal iterations, determines complexity and number of resonant bands

scaling_factor.name: Scaling Factor
scaling_factor.description: Geometric scaling ratio between successive iterations

base_length.name: Base Triangle Side Length
base_length.description: Side length of the largest triangle in the fractal structure

wire_radius.name: Wire Radius
wire_radius.description: Radius of the wire elements forming the fractal structure

feed_gap.name: Feed Gap
feed_gap.description: Gap width at the feed point of the base triangle

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining the fractal geometry parameters, calculating resonant frequencies for each iteration level, optimizing the scaling factor for desired frequency ratios, and adjusting feed structure for impedance matching across multiple bands.

steps.title: Determine Base Frequency and Triangle Size
steps.description: Calculate the base triangle dimensions for the lowest operating frequency

steps.title: Select Iteration Level
steps.description: Choose fractal iteration level based on desired number of operating bands

steps.title: Calculate Scaling Factor
steps.description: Determine scaling factor for desired frequency spacing between bands

steps.title: Generate Fractal Geometry
steps.description: Create successive iterations using the scaling factor

steps.title: Calculate Resonant Frequencies
steps.description: Determine resonant frequencies for each iteration level

steps.title: Design Feed Structure
steps.description: Optimize feed gap and matching network for multiband operation

steps.title: Impedance Matching
steps.description: Adjust wire radius and feed structure for acceptable VSWR across bands

equations.radiationPattern: E(θ,φ) = Σ I_n * sin(k * L_n * cos(θ)) / (k * L_n * cos(θ))

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use method of moments with triangular basis functions for wire segments, include mutual coupling between fractal elements
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Superposition of radiation from all wire segments with appropriate phase relationships, account for fractal self-similarity
keyAssumptions: Thin wire approximation valid for a << λ
Fractal elements are electrically small at lowest frequency
Feed point located at base of largest triangle

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 14
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 20
Werner, D.H. 'Fractal Antenna Engineering Handbook', Artech House
Puente, C. 'Fractal Antennas: A Novel Antenna Miniaturization Technique', IEEE Antennas and Propagation Magazine
